body.title: /decided skill: always file verification ticket when behavioral hypothesis present
body.result: updated Step 5.5 skip condition + gate logic; S-only+hypothesis batches now get a consequence-check ticket gated on last ticket
body.description:
The /decided skill's Step 5.5 only generates a consequence-check ticket for M/L/XL batches. But when a design extracts a behavioral hypothesis (Q2: 'what should be observably different') via Step 2.6, the decision has a testable claim that needs a verification gate regardless of ticket size. Yesterday's D-target-difficulty-tiers filed 3 S-size tickets with a clear behavioral hypothesis but no verification ticket — and the end-state was never reached because nobody checked.

Fix Step 5.5 in /decided: change the skip condition from 'every ticket in batch is S' to 'every ticket is S AND no behavioral hypothesis was extracted in Step 2.6.' If Q2/Q3 answers are present, always file a verification ticket gated on the last ticket in the batch.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/decided/ (skill file edit)
**Design rules:** none apply — this is a process document
**Scope boundary:** IN: Step 5.5 skip condition update; verification ticket template when hypothesis present. OUT: Step 2.6 itself; consequence ticket content (unchanged)
**Test plan:** Manual verification — next /decided run with S-only batch + behavioral hypothesis produces a verification ticket; S-only batch with no hypothesis (pure schema change, no behavioral claim) still skips
